title: DFL UX Paths v1
description: Versioned per-app user-flow JSON schema for migration-gap analysis and dead-code detection. Accepts schema_version 1.0.0, 1.1.0, and 1.2.0 (all additive — v1.1 adds optional navigation/prereq/test_metadata fields; v1.2 adds optional source_ref (screen→repo file) and screenshots[] (per platform×orientation), and documents `route` as the canonical shared route nomenclature for 1:1 cross-app mapping. Backward compatible — v1.0/v1.1 files still validate).
$comment: schema v1.2 — additive over v1.1. New optional screen fields: source_ref { component_file, file_path_chain? } links a screen to the repo file(s) that render it (Figma-Code-Connect style); screenshots[] { platform, orientation, viewport?, url, captured_at? } captures rendered images per platform×orientation for vision-first comparison. `screen.id` is the STICKY 1:1 JOIN KEY across apps — comparators pair screens by shared id. `route` is the canonical shared route nomenclature (e.g. studio/project/:id/editor) letting two apps map 1:1 by the same name even when their real URLs differ. Draft 2020-12. The file `$id` is intentionally unchanged across additive bumps so consumers pinned to the canonical URL keep working.

schema_version.description: Version of the dfl-ux-paths schema this file conforms to. v1.1.0 adds optional navigation_path, prerequisites, test_metadata, and richer flow step objects. v1.2.0 adds optional screen.source_ref (screen→repo file link) and screen.screenshots[] (per platform×orientation), and documents `route` as the canonical shared route nomenclature for 1:1 cross-app mapping.

app_version.description: App build identifier in the form YYYY-MM-DD-<git-sha-short>.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this snapshot was generated.

test_metadata.description: v1.1 — app-level metadata used by e2e-user-persona / Playwright skills to bootstrap a session (auth strategy, default viewport, base URL).

id.description: Stable screen identifier (snake_case recommended). REQUIRED. This is the STICKY 1:1 JOIN KEY across apps: cross-app comparators (web ↔ mobile, old build ↔ new build) pair screens by shared `id`. Treat it as a contract — keep ids stable across versions and identical across apps you intend to map 1:1, or diffs/parity comparisons break.

route.description: v1.2 — canonical SHARED route nomenclature for 1:1 cross-app mapping (e.g. 'studio/project/:id/editor'). Lets two apps (web ↔ mobile) map by the same route name even when their real URLs/deep-links differ. Also doubles as the screen's route/path/deep-link when the app uses a single URL scheme. Use ':param' for dynamic segments. Optional, additive.

source_ref.description: v1.2 — link from this screen to the repo file(s) that render it (route/page/modal component). Figma-Code-Connect style. Optional, additive — populated by the route→file resolver (bin/resolve-routes.mjs) or by hand.

screenshots.description: v1.2 — rendered screenshots of this screen, one entry per platform×orientation(×viewport). URLs are committed (e.g. S3 via supabase-upload) so humans AND agents view the same image for vision-first comparison. Optional, additive.

components.description: Component identifiers rendered on this screen.

api_calls.description: API endpoints or RPC calls invoked from this screen.

navigation_path.description: v1.1 — ordered list of UI actions to navigate TO this screen from the app entrypoint. Used by e2e-user-persona to auto-generate persona scripts instead of LLM-driven discovery. When multiple navigationStep entries are present, the runner tries them in order and the first matching selector wins (useful for resilience across renames).

prerequisites.description: v1.1 — preconditions that must hold for this screen to be reachable / functional.

sourceRef.description: v1.2 — points a screen at the repo file(s) that render it.

component_file.description: Repo-relative path to the route/page/modal component that renders this screen (e.g. 'app/studio/[id]/editor.tsx' for Expo Router, 'app/studio/[id]/editor/page.tsx' for Next app-router). This is what the route→file resolver emits.

file_path_chain.description: Optional — ordered repo-relative paths from the app entry (e.g. 'App.tsx' / 'app/_layout.tsx') down to the component, giving the 'tree' from app root to this screen's renderer.

screenshot.description: v1.2 — one rendered screenshot of a screen, keyed by platform×orientation(×viewport).

platform.description: Rendering platform the screenshot was captured on.

viewport.description: Optional viewport dimensions string, e.g. '375x812' or '1440x900'.

url.description: Public URL of the committed screenshot (e.g. S3 via supabase-upload).

captured_at.description: Optional ISO-8601 timestamp when the screenshot was captured.

next_screen.description: Screen id the user lands on after this action.

side_effect.description: Free-form description of side effect (mutation, analytics, etc.).

start.description: Screen id where the flow starts.

oneOf.description: v1.0 — screen id traversed in this flow.

steps.description: Ordered list of flow steps. v1.0 accepts plain screen ids (strings); v1.1 additionally accepts step objects with action/selector/target_screen for executable scripts. Mixed arrays are allowed during migration.

actions.description: Ordered list of action ids exercised during this flow.

tested_by.description: References to tests (file paths, test names) that cover this flow.

screen.description: Screen id where this step is executed.

action.description: Action verb (tap, type, scroll, swipe, navigate, wait, assert, etc.).

value.description: Optional value to type / select / assert.

target_screen.description: Screen id the user lands on after this step.

wait_for_target.description: Selector / condition to wait for to confirm the target screen rendered.

wait_for.description: Selector / condition to wait for after performing the action to confirm success.

auth_required.description: True if a logged-in user session is required to reach this screen.

auth_role.description: Optional — specific role required (e.g. 'admin', 'instructor'). Omit for any authenticated user.

data_required.description: Free-form descriptions of DB/state preconditions (e.g. 'lesson_studio.projects has >= 0 rows').

preconditions_in_app.description: Free-form descriptions of in-app preconditions (feature flag enabled, onboarding complete, etc.).

feature_flags.description: Feature flag identifiers that must be enabled.

auth_strategy.description: Brief description of how to authenticate (e.g. 'supabase-anon + CLAUDE_SMOKE creds', 'magic-link', 'oauth google').

default_viewport.description: Hint for test runners — preferred viewport for this app.

test_user_secret_path.description: Infisical path to credentials suitable for smoke tests (e.g. '/shared/CLAUDE_SMOKE_USERNAME').

notes.description: Free-form notes for test authors / persona-script generators.
